name: Church of the Nativity
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site marking the traditional birthplace of Jesus Christ, this ancient basilica was originally commissioned in 327 AD. It is one of the oldest continuously operating churches in the world, featuring Crusader-era mosaics and the Grotto of the Nativity beneath the altar.

name: Al-Aqsa Mosque
description: The third holiest site in Islam, located on the Haram al-Sharif in the Old City of Jerusalem. The silver-domed mosque has stood as a center of Islamic worship and learning for over thirteen centuries and remains a powerful symbol of faith and heritage.

name: Church of the Holy Sepulchre
description: Revered by Christians as the site of the crucifixion, burial, and resurrection of Jesus, this church in the Old City's Christian Quarter draws pilgrims from across the globe. Its history stretches back to the 4th century and it is shared by several Christian denominations.

name: Dome of the Rock
description: An iconic Islamic shrine with its unmistakable golden dome, completed in 691 AD on the Haram al-Sharif. It is one of the oldest works of Islamic architecture and enshrines the Foundation Stone, sacred to multiple faiths.

name: Ibrahimi Mosque (Cave of the Patriarchs)
description: One of the oldest continuously used prayer structures in the world, believed to be the burial place of the Patriarch Abraham and his family. This monumental Herodian-era enclosure is sacred to Muslims, Jews, and Christians alike.

name: Shepherds' Field
description: The traditional site near Beit Sahour where, according to the Gospel, angels announced the birth of Jesus to shepherds tending their flocks. The peaceful grounds include a chapel and offer a contemplative atmosphere with views over the surrounding hills.

name: Milk Grotto
description: A soft white chalk cave chapel a short walk from the Church of the Nativity, venerated by Christian tradition as a resting place of the Holy Family. Pilgrims have visited this serene shrine for centuries seeking blessings.

name: Mar Saba Monastery
description: A dramatic Greek Orthodox monastery clinging to the cliffs of the Kidron Valley in the Judean Desert, founded in 483 AD. It is one of the oldest inhabited monasteries in the world and offers breathtaking desert scenery.

name: Tower of David Museum
description: An ancient citadel near the Jaffa Gate that tells the 2,700-year story of Jerusalem through archaeological remains and immersive exhibits. Its ramparts offer some of the finest panoramic views over the Old City.

name: Hisham's Palace
description: An early Islamic Umayyad-era desert palace dating to the 8th century, famed for its exquisite 'Tree of Life' mosaic and elaborate stonework. The site is a remarkable window into the artistic achievements of the period.

name: Tell es-Sultan (Ancient Jericho)
description: Widely considered one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities on earth, with archaeological layers stretching back more than 10,000 years. This UNESCO-listed mound reveals Neolithic towers and walls of extraordinary antiquity.

name: Sebastia Archaeological Site
description: A layered archaeological site set among olive groves, showcasing Roman columns, a forum, an amphitheater, and remains spanning Iron Age to Byzantine periods. It was once the capital of the ancient Kingdom of Israel.

name: Solomon's Pools
description: Three monumental ancient reservoirs that once supplied water to Jerusalem and Bethlehem through an elaborate aqueduct system. Surrounded by greenery, the pools are an impressive feat of ancient engineering.

name: Roman Cardo
description: The excavated main thoroughfare of Roman and Byzantine Jerusalem, lined with reconstructed columns that evoke the bustle of the ancient city. Today it blends archaeology with artisan shops in the Jewish Quarter.

name: Battir Agricultural Terraces
description: A UNESCO World Heritage cultural landscape of ancient stone terraces irrigated by a Roman-era system still used by local families. The lush valley is ideal for gentle walks amid centuries of living agricultural heritage.

name: Old City of Nablus
description: A vibrant historic quarter of winding alleys, Ottoman architecture, traditional soap factories, and bustling markets. It is the beating heart of Nablus culture and the birthplace of the city's world-famous knafeh.

name: Khan al-Wakala
description: A beautifully restored Ottoman-era caravanserai that once hosted merchants and traders along regional routes. Its elegant courtyard and arches now host cultural events and offer a glimpse into the city's commercial past.

name: Ramallah Cultural Palace
description: A leading modern venue for Palestinian arts, hosting theater, music, dance, and film throughout the year. It is a hub of contemporary cultural life and a showcase for local and international performances.

name: Dar Zahran Heritage Building
description: A lovingly preserved 250-year-old stone house turned heritage museum and gallery, documenting Ramallah's social history through photographs and artifacts. It regularly hosts art exhibitions and cultural gatherings.

name: The Palestinian Museum
description: An award-winning contemporary museum dedicated to Palestinian history, culture, and society, set amid terraced gardens. Its rotating exhibitions and striking architecture make it a flagship cultural destination.

name: Hebron Old City & Glass Workshops
description: A historic market district renowned for its covered souks, traditional glass and ceramic workshops, and centuries-old Mamluk and Ottoman architecture. Artisans here continue crafts passed down through generations.

name: Wadi Qelt
description: A dramatic desert canyon running between Jerusalem and Jericho, dotted with springs, ancient aqueducts, and the cliff-hanging St. George's Monastery. It is a favorite for hikers seeking striking wilderness scenery.

name: Mount of Temptation
description: A towering desert mountain overlooking Jericho, reachable by a scenic cable car and home to a Greek Orthodox monastery built into the cliff. The summit rewards visitors with sweeping views of the Jordan Valley.

name: Wadi al-Badhan
description: A green valley of natural springs and shaded picnic spots northeast of Nablus, popular with families escaping the summer heat. Its flowing water and lush vegetation make it a beloved local retreat.

name: Al-Aqsa Sweets (Knafeh)
description: An iconic sweet shop serving Nablus's legendary knafeh, a warm dessert of stretchy cheese, semolina pastry, and sweet syrup topped with pistachios. Tasting it fresh is an essential Palestinian culinary experience.

name: Rukab Ice Cream
description: A beloved Ramallah institution since 1941, famous for its uniquely chewy Arabic ice cream made with mastic and sahlab. Its bustling parlor is a cherished social stop for locals and visitors alike.

name: Qalqilya Zoo
description: The only zoo in Palestine, home to a variety of animals and a natural history museum, offering a fun and educational outing for children and families. Its shaded grounds make for a relaxed day out.

name: Bethlehem Peace Center
description: A cultural center on Manger Square hosting exhibitions, a museum, and events that celebrate the heritage of Bethlehem. Its central location makes it an easy and engaging family stop in the heart of the city.
